notes: Smallest tool-less round-trip isolating the TERMINAL-TEXT end signal for kiro-cli: one user prompt produces a final text-only AssistantMessage (no toolUse) and the parser maps it to turn_done, settling working→ready via rule 2b. Overlaps basic-turn intentionally — this cell pins the end-of-turn mechanism, not the broader lifecycle. WHY interactive (not headless --no-interactive): headless kiro-cli writes NO session file under ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/ and is invisible to the daemon (FINDINGS.md §7), so the TUI (kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools) is mandatory; the REPL must outlive the observation window so the working→ready settle is observed as a distinct arc, held alive by the trailing sleep before the single tmux kill. LAZY TRANSCRIPT / SESSION IDENTITY: kiro writes its <uuid>.jsonl lazily (only after the first prompt lands), so the on-time ready birth is the pre-session (proc-<PID>) row and the UUID-keyed session reconciles in on first input; the working/ready flip then plays out on the UUID session. turn_end anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready. WAITING NEGATIVE INVARIANT: the parser sets ev.AssistantText from the last text block; IsWaitingForUserInput diverts to waiting only on a trailing question mark or imperative cue, and the recipe elicits a declarative answer with no trailing ? and no cue, so the turn closes on the ready path and never enters waiting — kiro lands ready (not waiting) after a plain statement turn. TOKENS/COST/MODEL: the kiro-cli JSONL carries none (the <uuid>.json sidecar holds credits + model_id but input/output_token_count are 0, and the parser does not read it), so observations assert tokens_nonzero=false, cost_nonzero=false, model unknown. The scenario asserts no token criterion, so this does not weaken the state-arc verdict. Tool calls ≤ 0 (no toolUse blocks). Final state = ready.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
UUID session reconciles (distinct session_id) after the lazy transcript appears, born ready
PID bound to the UUID session
User prompt drives ready → working on the UUID session (the single tool-less assistant turn)
Terminal text-only AssistantMessage with no tool call closes the turn → back to ready; state never entered waiting
